delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: Englishes, primarily from the outer circle of English. The first introductory unit will consider the great variety of NNS English with a focus on identifying different accents and features of Asian Englishes (Singlish, Hinglish). The second unit will examine the distinction between dialect, language and creoles. The third unit will address NNS English language in Africa and the Caribbean.
aims: To introduce participants to the rich diversity of Non-Native Speaker (NNS) Englishes, with a specific emphasis on identifying different accents and linguistic features present in Asian Englishes such as Singlish and Hinglish. Participants will develop an understanding of the linguistic characteristics that define these varieties.
goals: To equip participants with the skills necessary for effective cross-cultural communication in diverse English-speaking contexts.
To encourage participants to critically analyze prevailing attitudes and stereotypes associated with NNS Englishes.
To explore the conceptual distinctions between dialects, languages, and creoles within the context of NNS Englishes.

plan.text: Introduction to themes to be covered in semester and distribution of reading material.
Discussion of what constitutes a "World English". Is English spoken in Japan considered a World English. What is necessary for such a definition?
Unit 1.The great variety of NNS English with a focus on identifying different accents and features of Asian Englishes (Singlish, Hinglish).
Unit 2. The distinction between dialect, language and creoles.
Unit 3. NNS English language in Africa and the Caribbean.

textbooks: All materials will be provided by the teacher.
prereq: Students should have a reasonable understanding of English to comprehend a seminar held in English.
prep: Students will be required to review content of seminars outside of class and to read in preparation for future seminars.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
